title: Closing Keynote by Richard Sutton: Welcome to the Era of Experience

not here to introduce him. I'm here to

Shelley, thank you so much. You did a

So, um it is my great honor to introduce

my life, whoever you are listening to us
here now, that is unaffected by the work
of this eminent computer scientist. And
that goes not just for us, but for our

Reinforcement learning
in all its profuse variations is today
the universal tool of artificial
intelligence.
And no one deserves more credit for its
success than Richard Sutton.
Like everything else in computer
science, it seems reinforcement learning
was anticipated by Alan Turring.
Even before his provocative
computing machinery and intelligence
paper, Turring put it quite simply. What
we want, he said, is a machine that can
learn from experience.
But it took a man with a psychology
degree from Stanford to think about how
to build on what was known about animal
training and recreate it for the realm
of machines.
Starting in the 1970s, Sutton, working
with his PhD adviser at UMass Amherst,
Andrew Barto,
worked out what that meant in
mathematical terms.
He persisted through AI winters and
summers, bubbles and bursts,
and through orders of magnitude changes
in computing power and equally drastic
changes in computing models.
Sutton not only formulated the key
concepts and tools but wrote the
definitive textbook through which he
shared them with the world.
To make a long story short, Sutton along
with BTO was awarded the touring award
in 2025,
only the crown jewel of many other
recognitions
he has received.
He joins us today from the University of
Alberta where he is professor and also a
technological entrepreneur.
Professor Sutton, we are honored to have
you with us and thank you for sharing
your wisdom.
[applause]
Thank you, Harry. It's my great honor to
speak to you all today at your AI
summit.
Um
it's kind of hard to live up to all the

so I'm going to talk about uh the era of

now

That is absolutely static. Is absolutely

Uh and I think we're starting to reach
